text: Why is "TransBerry" in a kids show on Netflix?
embedded_text:
The Strawberry Shortcake show has a character who is a "TransBerry"

What is wrong with these people? Why do they insist on corrupting kids with their degeneracy?

To be clear, these activists don't just want to be "left alone," they're coming for your kids unless we stop it.

text:
To the NGOs funded by the Benioff tax, the drug dealers are a feature, not a bug. 

The NGOs get paid close to a million dollars for each drug zombie they "manage". Their incentive is therefore massive to keep the narcotics dealers operating, otherwise their herd of drug zombies will leave.

You may have noticed how easily the drug zombies were removed from SF when President Xi came to town. Doesn't it seem odd that there were literally zero drug zombies in SF when he visited? Zero.

text: Earth's gravity is strong enough to make reaching Mars extremely hard, but not impossible

Why Super-Earthlings Might Never Reach the Stars

In the rocket equation, the fuel required to reach orbit grows exponentially with gravity. If Earth's gravity were 15% stronger, space programs would likely be impossible. https://t.co/wIY5bqtJBn

text:
I am confident that Starship will land humans on Mars. That path is clear. 

But what really matters is securing the future of consciousness, not just getting a small number of people to Mars. 

That probably requires getting over 100,000 people and 1M tons of cargo to Mars. 

The critical threshold to pass is making it such that Mars can grow even if supply ships from Earth stop coming for any reason.

hot tip:

before you sign any legal forms, dump the who thing into Grok and ask it to "read this for me and tell me what I should know before signing"

Grok isn't a lawyer (yet) but neither are you, and let's admit you skim anyway and thus expose yourself to a lot of risk https://t.co/E5FwGrrgUn

That is biologically impossible. 

Great credit to @jk_rowling for her stalwart fight against this incredibly destructive mind virus.

To the degree that people are seeing improvements in their feed, it is not due to the actions of specific individuals changing heuristics, but rather increasing use of Grok and other AI tools. 

We will post the updated algorithm, including model weights, later this week. You will see that a lot of random vestigial rules are now gone. 

Going to full AI recommendations, where all 100M+ posts per day are evaluated by Grok and those most likely to interest you are recommended happens next month. This will profoundly improve the quality of your feed. 

Shortly after that, we will build the ability for you to dynamically adjust your recommended content, eg "show me less politics".

text: So few understand the implications, even when stated as clearly as below
embedded_text:
Rocket reusability isn't just an optimization - it's a phase transition.

When you throw rockets away, you're limited by factory throughput (~16 rockets/year). Reuse them, and you enter a different regime of launch volume, economics, and ambition. https://t.co/sLxc83Ds0q

Go to Settings → Enable "Open App in Voice Mode"

embedded_text:
If Indians set foot in England and become English.

Then the English who set foot in India became Indian.

Therefore the English did not rule India.

There is no such thing as 'colonization.'

I was quite literally radicalized by 60 Minutes.

I used to be like so many Americans, watching it religiously and trusting it.

But then as a young man I watched them do a report on something for which I am an expert.

Their reporting was wildly inaccurate and was obviously done

Multi-planet civilization offered a new evolution path that is not constrained by a single environment nor culture.

Like RL, we often place an agent in vastly different environments (or gyms) to generalize, and often exceed, their skills learned from a single environment.

Let's be very clear.

The reason that the mainstream media ignores the threat of the far-left is because the media are part of the far-left.

Don't believe their propaganda.

Grok made our vacation/road trip better. 

It found us fun things to visit. And good things to eat.

And answered some science questions to settle family arguments.

– Driver Profile Sloth :sloth:

text:
Many more Tesla self-driving V14 improvements still to come, but wide rollout of V14.1 has begun. 

14.2 rolls out in a few weeks and then 14.3 a few weeks later, depending on safety testing.

There is so much change that we are carefully confirming each one.

{'inline_media': []}, 'richtext': {'richtext_tags': []}, 'text': 'FSD 14 first impressions: the good: - destination options are incredible and very well done. parking has been fast and flawless so far, zero corrections and right between the lines. also pulls into the driveways of houses very reliably and actually parks itself, feels like the freakin' future - V14 is always READY TO GO, if your car is in gear there is absolutely zero delay from pressing the "Self-Driving" button to when it actually takes off. feels like it's been considering what to do for a lifetime and you finally let it free lol, SUPER fast - it squeezes gaps that V13 would never go for (see my narrow gate video for an example) and also just generally feels like it's never wondering what to do, it's always executing a maneuver and not pausing like V13 - speed profiles feel pretty different now (in a good way), although I'm pretty sure the new "sloth" mode just lowers the max speed to the detected speed limit which artificially limits it (sometimes feels like it wants to drive faster even in sloth mode but can't) - there's a particular wide road with a 25mph speed limit near me that V13 would speed 10+ mph on very consistently, V14 did 5mph over on "standard" profile which is a big improvement - it seems like it tries to stay within the lane lines much better than V13, which is technically the correct and legal thing to do but can sometimes make it feel slightly more jerky during turning. I had the same feedback in my Robotaxi rides. overall a positive though, it's definitely staying further away from curbs and not cutting corners like V13 sometimes would - U-turns have absolutely never been handled better, I did a bunch for testing and it handled them all flawlessly even in the dark where the lights didn't light up some of the drivable space. felt like it remembered what it saw from the headlights in the moments before and just went for it - I'm not sure if they updated the maps, but several places where the car did the wrong thing on V13 (like use wrong turning lane for incorrect address in navigation), V14 no longer does. I had 3 spots I was super excited to test and it passed 2/3 (the one that wasn't fixed wasn't a disengagement, it just went around the block and did a U-turn that was not necessary) - I feel like I have not gotten even close to stress testing this, which I feel like I have to point out in the "good" section because man, it's crazy how far we've come. I was thinking about this a lot during the drive as it went through several areas where older versions would struggle with every time - the car feels much more sentient, as others have described. you can definitely feel V14 "considering" a lot more when it's driving about its surroundings which is usually a great thing, but sometimes not (which I will explain below) the bad: - had two minor braking events that happened very quickly (was probably only braking a total of 1/10 of a second) but felt pretty harsh. like a quick jab of the brakes before going back to regular driving. both times it did this I was messing with the navigation route, but still startled me a bit - had a moment where it tried to pull over to the side of the road for a moment due to someone approaching us behind with super blue headlights. it never actually pulled over, but signaled and started to go off the road, then immediately changed its mind and continued - I chose two impossible destinations, the first was behind a fence and it just reversed all the way back out into the road and did several u-turns before arriving at the same spot each time, stuck in an infinite loop. the second was a destination pin slightly behind a closed road where it technically could have gone around, but didn't and instead stopped at the nearest stop sign and shifted itself into park (better than an infinite loop I suppose) - there's a new message on screen that says "Increased attention required" that pops up whenever you approach a construction zone or RR crossing (possibly for other stuff too), but I feel like it's actually distracting. it appears suddenly and makes me take my eyes off the road to see what it says, which is probably the opposite of what they want. I think a subtle blue glow like before or maybe a sound would be better overall, super impressed and can't wait to actually stress test tomorrow. seems like @tesla_ai absolutely cooked here and it seriously feels like a robotaxi with some of the features they've added. they've fixed SO many things in my local area, which I'll talk about in my first impressions video gn everyone'}}}, 'rest_id': '1975525268195582250', 'source': 'Twitter Web App', 'unmention_data': {}, 'views': {'count': '9808316', 'state': 'EnabledWithCount'}}}, 'rest_id': '1975611643888541986', 'source': 'Twitter for iPhone', 'unmention_data': {}, 'views': {'count': '9498191', 'state': 'EnabledWithCount'}}

And much more still to come. By V14.3, your car will feel like it is sentient.

10 drives in with FSD v14.1 and here are all my thoughts. 

First of all- wow. Zero disengagements or interventions thus far. The confidence and overall human like driving is next level. The steering inputs are so smooth, braking inputs are earlier and more linear than before. https://t.co/sz2cgqJhyv

First of all- wow. Zero disengagements or interventions thus far. The confidence and overall human like driving is next level. The steering inputs are so smooth, braking inputs are earlier and more linear than before. It gives off Robotaxi vibes with how it navigates parking lots, plus how it drives and how quickly it backs into a parking spot. Such a nice experience to ride along with it. Seeing FSD now navigate parking garages is so cool. When you enter it waits for the arm to open after you get your ticket, proceeds in and finds a parking space. When you leave it does the same thing, gets close to the ticket stand and then proceeds. Really fascinating to see in person. It really sometimes feels like FSD can now read signs in the garage to guide it to the exit. Brought it to the back corner of a garage and it found its way out. Hurry mode is awesome, quick and more assertive than before, while also being smoother with less unnecessary lane changes. Sloth mode is what you’d expect- exact speed limit and gentle driving. Icons are cool and easy to change. When you start FSD from park, there’s no delay. It starts right away and leaves in a split second. Really good improvement. I love being able to select the Arrival Options if I am parking curbside or in a charger, it also automatically chooses depending on where you navigate to. It perfectly parks at Superchargers, tried it at the Tesla Diner and at the Santa Monica supercharger and both times it was excellent. Always centered in the lines and it parks better and quicker than the majority of humans do. Only thing so far to note is one instance of slight braking when going around a bus blocking a lane, was probably not even 1/2 of a second and dropped 1-2mph but felt it. Super super minor. Had FSD v14 move over for construction, a loose cone on a dark road, and obey a worker holding a stop sign. All of them were smooth and felt human like or better. It pulls in and out of my driveway great, doesn’t hesitate at all as well as when at chargers. Everything has a quicker response time. Curbside is cool too as it pulls right up against the curb perfectly in a parking spot. This release now means now 100% of your driving can now be done on FSD. From your driveway into a parking garage. Elon was right when he said it would feel sentient, and this isn’t even v14.2 yet. The Tesla AI team COOKED with this update. It’s phenomenal. Huge congrats to them for such an epic release. This is a HUGE update and I can’t wait to drive it more. It’s now 5am so I’m going to attempt to get some sleep, but tons more driving and videos coming later today as soon as I can. Thanks everyone for following along.'}}}, 'rest_id': '1975530950500229202', 'source': 'Twitter for iPhone', 'unmention_data': {}, 'views': {'count': '12284513', 'state': 'EnabledWithCount'}}}, 'rest_id': '1975610840171917435', 'source': 'Twitter for iPhone', 'unmention_data': {}, 'views': {'count': '11817517', 'state': 'EnabledWithCount'}}

- They funded and infiltrated the Judges.

If you want this to stop, you have to tackle all three and you better do it quick.

text: OpenAI is built on a lie
embedded_text:
Every so often, OpenAI employees ask me how I see the co now.

It's always tough to give a simple answer. Some things they're doing, eg on CoT monitoring or building out system cards, are great.

@moseskagan @micsolana Yes. The SF Prop C "gross receipts" tax applies to all transactions, not just revenue, which makes it impossible to operate a payments company in the city. The sheer insanity of that takes a moment to sink in …

That's why Stripe and Square had to leave SF.
